history.text: A short-lived independence period began, an early but unstable step in Dominican Republic's statehood.
First Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Second Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Third Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Fourth Republic introduced a new form of state rule.

worthSeeing.title: Punta Cana:
worthSeeing.text: All-inclusive beach resorts on a 50 km stretch of palm-lined Caribbean sand.

worthSeeing.title: Santo Domingo:
worthSeeing.text: The oldest continuously inhabited European city in the Americas, UNESCO listed.

worthSeeing.title: Merengue and Bachata:
worthSeeing.text: Two UNESCO-recognised music genres born on this island define Caribbean culture.

planningQuestions.q: What is Dominican Republic known for?
planningQuestions.a: Punta Cana, Santo Domingo, Merengue and Bachata.
